title: Progression from Joint Probability to Stochastic Processes
description: A visual flow showing how foundational probability concepts build up to form a Markov Chain, a specific type of stochastic process.
elements: Joint Probability Distribution (State at time t)
Markov Chain (A type of Stochastic Process)
relationships: A Joint Distribution describes a system's state at a given time. The Conditional Probability of the next state, given the current state, defines a transition model. If this model obeys the Markov Property, the system has no memory beyond its present state. This rule, applied over a State Space, generates a sequence of random states, which is a Stochastic Process. A Stochastic Process that follows the Markov Property is specifically called a Markov Chain.
